name: deep-research
description: Conduct enterprise-grade research with multi-source synthesis, citation tracking, and verification. Use when user needs comprehensive analysis requiring 10+ sources, verified claims, or comparison of approaches. Triggers include "deep research", "comprehensive analysis", "research report", "compare X vs Y", or "analyze trends". Do NOT use for simple lookups, debugging, or questions answerable with 1-2 searches.
capabilities: Executes 8-phase research pipeline: Scope, Plan, Retrieve, Triangulate, Synthesize, Critique, Refine, Package
Parallel search execution across 5-10 concurrent searches for faster results
Source credibility scoring (0-100) for academic, government, and technical domains
Citation verification via DOI resolver and URL accessibility checks
Generates markdown, HTML (McKinsey-style), and PDF report formats
author: 199-biotechnologies
securitySummary: Legitimate research automation skill using Python standard library only. Network access limited to DOI/URL verification for citation validation. File system access restricted to ~/.claude/research_output/ for state persistence. Static findings are false positives: markdown code blocks misclassified as Ruby execution, benign technical terms flagged as security keywords, legitimate verification URLs flagged as hardcoded URLs.
valueStatement: Need thorough, citation-backed research across multiple sources? This skill delivers verified, professional-grade reports through an 8-phase methodology with source credibility scoring and automatic citation validation. Stop spending hours searching and synthesizing. Get comprehensive analysis ready for decisions.
limitations: Requires internet connection for web searches and citation verification
Does not access private databases, paywalled content, or authenticated APIs
Report quality depends on publicly available web sources
Cannot verify citations in real-time during generation (post-generation validation only)

useCases.title: Technology evaluation
useCases.description: Compare frameworks, tools, or platforms with verified sources and structured analysis

useCases.title: Market research
useCases.description: Analyze industry trends, funding patterns, or competitive landscapes

useCases.title: Academic investigation
useCases.description: Synthesize scientific literature with citation tracking and credibility assessment

promptTemplates.title: Quick overview
promptTemplates.prompt: Use deep research in quick mode to explore [topic] and identify key sources

promptTemplates.title: Standard analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Use deep research to analyze [specific question] with 15+ verified sources

promptTemplates.title: Deep investigation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use deep research in deep mode to compare [A vs B] with full triangulation and critique

promptTemplates.title: UltraDeep report
promptTemplates.prompt: Use deep research in ultradeep mode to generate a comprehensive report on [topic]

bestPractices: Specify research mode (quick/standard/deep/ultradeep) based on time and depth needed
Frame questions clearly with context for better results
Review Phase 1 (Scope) output to ensure research aligns with your needs
Use citation numbers in reports to trace claims back to source material
antiPatterns: Using this skill for simple lookups that WebSearch can answer in one query
Asking for debugging help since this skill focuses on research, not code fixes
Expecting real-time updates on breaking news since research relies on indexed sources
Using for time-sensitive queries without specifying urgency in the research scope

faq.question: What modes are available and when should I use each?
faq.answer: Quick (2-5 min) for exploration, Standard (5-10 min) for most questions, Deep (10-20 min) for important decisions, UltraDeep (20-45 min) for comprehensive reports.

faq.question: How many sources does a typical report include?
faq.answer: Quick mode: 10+ sources, Standard: 15-30, Deep: 25-40, UltraDeep: 30-50+. All sources are scored for credibility.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with other Claude tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Use WebSearch and WebFetch first, then invoke deep research for synthesis. The skill integrates with Claude Code workflow.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using citation verification?
faq.answer: Citation verification only checks DOI/URL accessibility. It does not send your research data externally. Only minimal metadata is exchanged.

faq.question: Why did my report fail validation?
faq.answer: Common issues: missing bibliography entries, placeholders like TODO, broken internal links, or citations without matching sources. Fix and regenerate.

faq.question: How does this compare to standard Claude research?
faq.answer: This skill adds 8-phase methodology, source credibility scoring, citation validation, multiple output formats, and unlimited-length reports via auto-continuation.
